title: Mouthwatering Mutton Basque-French Lamb Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Basque-French inspired lamb tagine made with shio-koji-cured lamb, fresh vegetables, and a hint of rosemary. Perfect for a festive dinner or special occasion.

ingredients.name: Shio-koji-cured lamb shoulder
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality shio-koji blend for best results.

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Choose a good quality extra virgin olive oil.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Use a sweet onion such as Vidalia or Maui for best flavor.

ingredients.name: Carrots
ingredients.notes: Use a mix of orange and purple carrots for added color.

ingredients.name: Potatoes
ingredients.notes: Choose high-starch potatoes for best results.

ingredients.name: Rosemary
ingredients.notes: Use fresh rosemary for best flavor.

ingredients.name: Thyme
ingredients.notes: Use fresh thyme for best flavor.

ingredients.name: Lemon juice
ingredients.notes: Choose fresh lemons for best results.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher salt for best results.

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Use freshly ground black pepper for best results.

ingredients.name: Egg wash (beaten egg mixed with water)
ingredients.notes: Use an egg wash for a golden brown crust.

instructions: Preheat the tagine pot over medium heat.
Heat the olive oil in the tagine pot over medium heat.
Add the onion to the tagine pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the carrots and potatoes to the tagine pot and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the shio-koji-cured lamb shoulder to the tagine pot and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the rosemary, thyme, garlic, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper to the tagine pot and stir to combine.
Close the lid of the tagine pot and cook over low heat for 2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Use an egg wash to brush the crust of the lamb before serving.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh rosemary and a side of crusty bread.
